problem.title: Minimum Height Trees
problem.description: A tree is an undirected graph in which any two vertices are connected by exactly one path. Given n nodes and edges, find all roots that produce trees of minimum height.
assumptions: At most 2 nodes can be MHT roots.

example.explanation: Node 1 is the center. Repeatedly remove leaf nodes; remaining node(s) are roots of minimum height trees.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Topological Sort
solutions.description: Iteratively prune leaf nodes (degree 1) from the outside in, similar to topological sort, until 1 or 2 nodes remain — these are the centroids that minimize tree height. Each pruning round removes the current outermost layer, narrowing toward the center. The key insight is that the center(s) of the longest path in a tree always minimize the maximum depth when used as root.
